cnn: The primary feature-mixing mechanism in the repeated layers is convolution (e.g. nn.Conv1d, nn.Conv2d, nn.Conv3d), driving spatial or temporal feature extraction. The layer stacking may preserve a fixed spatial resolution or include downsampling/pooling stages.
isotropic_transformer: The primary feature-mixing mechanism in the repeated layers is attention, MLP-based token mixing, or similar token-mixing variants. The token count remains constant across all layers with no stage barriers, token-merging, or spatial-downsampling transitions, making overall depth highly flexible.
hierarchical_transformer: The primary feature-mixing mechanism in the repeated layers is attention, with token merging, pooling, or spatial downsampling transitions between fixed macro-stages. The sequence length or spatial resolution progressively decreases across stages, constraining layer distribution by stage layout.
